stem: In a right triangle, one acute angle has measure x°. The length of the hypotenuse is 17, and the length of the leg opposite the angle with measure x° is 8. What is the value of sin x°? (Note: figure not drawn to scale.)
rationale: In a right triangle, sine of an acute angle equals the length of the opposite leg divided by the length of the hypotenuse: sin x° = 8/17 ≈ 0.4706.
